input_row.title: Ensure that 'Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants' is set to 'Yes'
input_row.description: Require administrators or appropriately delegated users to create new tenants.
input_row.rationale: It is recommended to only allow an administrator to create new tenants. This prevent users from creating new Microsoft Entra ID or Azure AD B2C tenants and ensures that only authorized users are able to do so. Impact: Enforcing this setting will ensure that only authorized users are able to create new tenants.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID 3. Under Manage, select Users 4. Under Manage, select User settings 5. Ensure that Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants is set to Yes Audit from PowerShell Import-Module Microsoft.Graph.Identity.SignIns Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Policy.ReadWrite.Authorization' Get-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy | Select-Object -ExpandProperty DefaultUserRolePermissions | Format-List Review the "DefaultUserRolePermissions" section of the output. Ensure that AllowedToCreateTenants is not "True".
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID 3. Under Manage, select Users 4. Under Manage, select User settings 5. Set Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants to Yes 6. Click Save Remediate from PowerShell Import-Module Microsoft.Graph.Identity.SignIns Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Policy.ReadWrite.Authorization' Select-MgProfile -Name beta $params = @{ DefaultUserRolePermissions = @{ AllowedToCreateTenants = $false } } Update-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy -AuthorizationPolicyId  -BodyParameter $params References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/users- default-permissions 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/roles/permissions- reference#tenant-creator 3. https://blog.admindroid.com/disable-users-creating-new-azure-ad-tenants-in- microsoft-365/
input_row.impact: Enforcing this setting will ensure that only authorized users are able to create new tenants.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit process contains clear API and PowerShell commands for checking the policy settings related to tenant creation rights.
- The rationale emphasizes that this control can be assured through automated queries as per the guidance provided by the CIS benchmark and Microsoft documentation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_encryption_policy_restrict_non_admin_users

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

- Use the Azure Resource Manager (ARM) API or Azure CLI commands to query the policy settings for Microsoft Entra ID.
- Enumerate through the organization's Azure AD tenant configuration using the necessary API endpoints or PowerShell commands.

### Steps Involved:
1. **PowerShell Command:**
   - Import the necessary PowerShell module:
     ```powershell
     Import-Module Microsoft.Graph.Identity.SignIns
     Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Policy.ReadWrite.Authorization'
     ```
   - Query the existing tenant policy settings:
     ```powershell
     Get-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy | Select-Object -ExpandProperty DefaultUserRolePermissions | Format-List
     ```

2. **Check the Configuration:**
   - Validate the output from the above command to check the **AllowedToCreateTenants** property.

### Pass/Fail Criteria:
- **Pass:** The property **AllowedToCreateTenants** is set to false, effectively restricting non-admin users from creating new tenants.
- **Fail:** If the property **AllowedToCreateTenants** is true, indicating that non-admin users are permitted to create tenants.

### Example API Endpoint:
- The PowerShell commands effectively leverage the Microsoft Graph API which the Azure Active Directory uses for policy querying.

This approach ensures that compliance with the control of restricting non-admin user tenant creation is effectively audited using Azure APIs and is fully automated without manual intervention.
